---
title: "JSC370 Final Project Visualization"
format:
 html:
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: jsc370_final-venv

---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from pandas.api.types import is_numeric_dtype, is_string_dtype
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.express as px
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
from sklearn.preprocessing import MultiLabelBinarizer
import matplotlib.cm as cm
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
import seaborn as sns
import os
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score,mean_squared_error, r2_score, mean_absolute_error, f1_score, roc_auc_score
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier, DecisionTreeRegressor
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV, TimeSeriesSplit, train_test_split
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
from xgboost import XGBRegressor
```


```{python}
movies = pd.read_csv("data/processed/movies.csv")
casting = pd.read_csv("data/processed/casting.csv")
person = pd.read_csv("data/processed/person.csv")
person_movie_history = pd.read_csv("data/processed/person_movie_history.csv")
movies_enrich = pd.read_csv("data/processed/movies_enrich.csv")
```


```{python}
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

# ============================================================
# 1. MODIFY THESE COLUMN NAMES
# ============================================================

# Required ID columns
PID_COL = "pid"
MOVIE_ID_COL = "Movie ID"

# Optional label columns
NAME_COL = "Name"          # change if your person name column is different
JOB_COL = "Job"            # only likely exists in df_1
TITLE_COL = "Title"        # optional, only if df_1 has movie title

# Scatterplot 1: During Film Making
# UNCERTAIN: replace these with your real df_1 columns
DF1_X = "past_n_movies"
DF1_Y = "past_avg_rating"

# Scatterplot 2: Current Career
# UNCERTAIN: replace these with your real df_2 columns
DF2_X = "n_movies"
DF2_Y = "wavg_rating"


# ============================================================
# 2. Clean data for JSON export
# ============================================================

def make_json_safe(df):
    out = df.copy()

    # Convert datetime columns to string
    for col in out.columns:
        if pd.api.types.is_datetime64_any_dtype(out[col]):
            out[col] = out[col].astype(str)

    # Replace NaN / inf with None so JavaScript can read it
    out = out.replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan)
    out = out.where(pd.notna(out), None)

    return out


df1_safe = make_json_safe(df_1)
df2_safe = make_json_safe(df_2)

# Optional: keep only columns needed to reduce file size
df1_cols = [
    PID_COL, MOVIE_ID_COL, DF1_X, DF1_Y,
    NAME_COL, JOB_COL
]
if TITLE_COL in df1_safe.columns:
    df1_cols.append(TITLE_COL)

df1_cols = [c for c in df1_cols if c in df1_safe.columns]

df2_cols = [
    PID_COL, DF2_X, DF2_Y,
    NAME_COL
]
df2_cols = [c for c in df2_cols if c in df2_safe.columns]

df1_records = df1_safe[df1_cols].to_dict(orient="records")
df2_records = df2_safe[df2_cols].to_dict(orient="records")

# Movie dropdown options
movie_cols = [MOVIE_ID_COL]
if TITLE_COL in df1_safe.columns:
    movie_cols.append(TITLE_COL)

movie_options = (
    df1_safe[movie_cols]
    .drop_duplicates(subset=[MOVIE_ID_COL])
    .sort_values(MOVIE_ID_COL)
    .to_dict(orient="records")
)


# ============================================================
# 3. Create HTML file
# ============================================================

html = f"""
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Linked Scatterplots</title>
    <script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.2.min.js"></script>

    <style>
        body {{
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            margin: 24px;
        }}

        .controls {{
            margin-bottom: 16px;
        }}

        .plot-container {{
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
            gap: 24px;
        }}

        .plot-box {{
            border: 1px solid #ddd;
            border-radius: 8px;
            padding: 12px;
        }}

        select, button {{
            font-size: 14px;
            padding: 6px 10px;
            margin-right: 8px;
        }}

        #selected-info {{
            margin-top: 10px;
            font-weight: bold;
        }}

        @media (max-width: 900px) {{
            .plot-container {{
                grid-template-columns: 1fr;
            }}
        }}
    </style>
</head>

<body>

<h2>Linked Person Scatterplots</h2>

<div class="controls">
    <label for="movie-select">Select movie:</label>
    <select id="movie-select"></select>
    <button id="reset-highlight">Reset highlight</button>
    <div id="selected-info">Selected person: none</div>
</div>

<div class="plot-container">
    <div class="plot-box">
        <h3>During Film Making</h3>
        <div id="scatter1"></div>
    </div>

    <div class="plot-box">
        <h3>Current Career</h3>
        <div id="scatter2"></div>
    </div>
</div>

<script>
const df1 = {json.dumps(df1_records, allow_nan=False)};
const df2 = {json.dumps(df2_records, allow_nan=False)};
const movieOptions = {json.dumps(movie_options, allow_nan=False)};

const PID_COL = {json.dumps(PID_COL)};
const MOVIE_ID_COL = {json.dumps(MOVIE_ID_COL)};
const NAME_COL = {json.dumps(NAME_COL)};
const JOB_COL = {json.dumps(JOB_COL)};
const TITLE_COL = {json.dumps(TITLE_COL)};

const DF1_X = {json.dumps(DF1_X)};
const DF1_Y = {json.dumps(DF1_Y)};
const DF2_X = {json.dumps(DF2_X)};
const DF2_Y = {json.dumps(DF2_Y)};

let currentRows1 = [];
let currentRows2 = [];
let selectedPid = null;


// ============================================================
// Dropdown setup
// ============================================================

const movieSelect = document.getElementById("movie-select");

movieOptions.forEach(row => {{
    const option = document.createElement("option");
    option.value = row[MOVIE_ID_COL];

    if (row[TITLE_COL] !== undefined && row[TITLE_COL] !== null) {{
        option.textContent = `${{row[TITLE_COL]}} (${{row[MOVIE_ID_COL]}})`;
    }} else {{
        option.textContent = row[MOVIE_ID_COL];
    }}

    movieSelect.appendChild(option);
}});


// ============================================================
// Helper functions
// ============================================================

function safeValue(row, col) {{
    return row[col] === undefined || row[col] === null ? "" : row[col];
}}

function makeCustomData(row, movieId=null) {{
    // This is the object you receive when a point is clicked.
    // You can add more fields here.
    return {{
        pid: row[PID_COL],
        movie_id: movieId,
        name: safeValue(row, NAME_COL),
        job: safeValue(row, JOB_COL),
        x_value: null,
        y_value: null
    }};
}}

function makeHoverText(row, xCol, yCol, movieId=null) {{
    const name = safeValue(row, NAME_COL);
    const job = safeValue(row, JOB_COL);

    return `
        <b>${{name}}</b><br>
        pid: ${{safeValue(row, PID_COL)}}<br>
        movie id: ${{movieId === null ? "" : movieId}}<br>
        job: ${{job}}<br>
        ${{xCol}}: ${{safeValue(row, xCol)}}<br>
        ${{yCol}}: ${{safeValue(row, yCol)}}
    `;
}}

function makeMarkerArrays(rows) {{
    const colors = rows.map(row => {{
        return String(row[PID_COL]) === String(selectedPid)
            ? "red"
            : "rgba(70, 120, 180, 0.65)";
    }});

    const sizes = rows.map(row => {{
        return String(row[PID_COL]) === String(selectedPid)
            ? 16
            : 8;
    }});

    const lineWidths = rows.map(row => {{
        return String(row[PID_COL]) === String(selectedPid)
            ? 3
            : 0.5;
    }});

    return {{ colors, sizes, lineWidths }};
}}

function makeTrace(rows, xCol, yCol, movieId=null) {{
    const markerArrays = makeMarkerArrays(rows);

    const customdata = rows.map(row => {{
        const cd = makeCustomData(row, movieId);
        cd.x_value = row[xCol];
        cd.y_value = row[yCol];
        return cd;
    }});

    return {{
        x: rows.map(row => row[xCol]),
        y: rows.map(row => row[yCol]),
        mode: "markers",
        type: "scatter",
        text: rows.map(row => safeValue(row, NAME_COL)),
        customdata: customdata,
        hovertext: rows.map(row => makeHoverText(row, xCol, yCol, movieId)),
        hoverinfo: "text",
        marker: {{
            color: markerArrays.colors,
            size: markerArrays.sizes,
            line: {{
                color: "black",
                width: markerArrays.lineWidths
            }}
        }}
    }};
}}

function getClickedPid(eventData) {{
    if (!eventData || !eventData.points || eventData.points.length === 0) {{
        return null;
    }}

    const custom = eventData.points[0].customdata;
    return custom.pid;
}}

function updateSelectedInfo() {{
    const info = document.getElementById("selected-info");

    if (selectedPid === null) {{
        info.textContent = "Selected person: none";
        return;
    }}

    const row =
        currentRows1.find(r => String(r[PID_COL]) === String(selectedPid)) ||
        currentRows2.find(r => String(r[PID_COL]) === String(selectedPid));

    const name = row ? safeValue(row, NAME_COL) : "";
    info.textContent = `Selected person: ${{name}} | pid: ${{selectedPid}}`;
}}

function updateHighlightOnly() {{
    const marker1 = makeMarkerArrays(currentRows1);
    const marker2 = makeMarkerArrays(currentRows2);

    Plotly.restyle("scatter1", {{
        "marker.color": [marker1.colors],
        "marker.size": [marker1.sizes],
        "marker.line.width": [marker1.lineWidths]
    }});

    Plotly.restyle("scatter2", {{
        "marker.color": [marker2.colors],
        "marker.size": [marker2.sizes],
        "marker.line.width": [marker2.lineWidths]
    }});

    updateSelectedInfo();
}}


// ============================================================
// Main rendering function
// ============================================================

function renderForMovie(movieId) {{
    selectedPid = null;

    currentRows1 = df1.filter(row => String(row[MOVIE_ID_COL]) === String(movieId));

    const moviePids = new Set(
        currentRows1.map(row => String(row[PID_COL]))
    );

    // df_2 is filtered to the people involved in the selected movie.
    // This assumes df_2 has one row per person or career-level person records.
    currentRows2 = df2.filter(row => moviePids.has(String(row[PID_COL])));

    const trace1 = makeTrace(currentRows1, DF1_X, DF1_Y, movieId);
    const trace2 = makeTrace(currentRows2, DF2_X, DF2_Y, movieId);

    const layout1 = {{
        xaxis: {{ title: DF1_X }},
        yaxis: {{ title: DF1_Y }},
        margin: {{ t: 20, r: 20, b: 60, l: 70 }},
        height: 500
    }};

    const layout2 = {{
        xaxis: {{ title: DF2_X }},
        yaxis: {{ title: DF2_Y }},
        margin: {{ t: 20, r: 20, b: 60, l: 70 }},
        height: 500
    }};

    Plotly.react("scatter1", [trace1], layout1, {{ responsive: true }});
    Plotly.react("scatter2", [trace2], layout2, {{ responsive: true }});

    updateSelectedInfo();
}


// ============================================================
// Events
// ============================================================

document.getElementById("scatter1").on("plotly_click", function(eventData) {{
    selectedPid = getClickedPid(eventData);
    updateHighlightOnly();
}});

document.getElementById("scatter2").on("plotly_click", function(eventData) {{
    selectedPid = getClickedPid(eventData);
    updateHighlightOnly();
}});

movieSelect.addEventListener("change", function() {{
    renderForMovie(movieSelect.value);
}});

document.getElementById("reset-highlight").addEventListener("click", function() {{
    selectedPid = null;
    updateHighlightOnly();
}});


// Initial render
if (movieOptions.length > 0) {{
    movieSelect.value = movieOptions[0][MOVIE_ID_COL];
    renderForMovie(movieSelect.value);
}}
</script>

</body>
</html>
"""

Path("linked_scatterplots.html").write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")

print("Saved: linked_scatterplots.html")

```
